ELPAC Level 3
NWEA 650–1000L
DTR  |  Listen to a Classroom Conversation
LCC-HS11-L3-6501000-HSSCIENG1112-MKT-NAV-01 • HS Science: Engineering Design 11-12 • Engineering Design Criteria and Constraints

The Culvert Hearing

Click  🎧  to listen.
Listen to a mock trial in an engineering class. A teacher is acting as the judge, and a student is acting as the attorney for one team. Listen carefully. You will hear the conversation only once. After listening, you will answer some questions.
Listen to the Conversation
1QUESTION 1: What is the attorney's objection fundamentally about?
2QUESTION 2: Why does the attorney ask the court to add to the rules instead of throwing them out?
3QUESTION 3: What does the attorney's statement that 'the design is solving the wrong problem' reveal about how engineering rules should be written?
Choose the best answer for each question. You may change your answers before submitting.
Answer all 3 questions to submit.
SMART SUPPORT:
Go back to the conversation. How does the way the speakers talk at the start tell you what kind of proceeding this is? How does the attorney's last answer shape what the design is really being judged on?
CA ELD PI.A.1, PI.A.3, PI.B.5, PI.B.6  |  HS-ETS1-1  |  HS Engineering Design 11-12: Criteria and Constraints
